title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is split between external risk reporting and new evaluation work probing where current models fail in high-stakes settings. The strongest updates focus on frontier-risk disclosure, conflict-sensitive deployment failures, interpretability-based auditing, privacy-preserving learning, and the reliability of chatbots as news intermediaries.

items.title: METR publishes Frontier Risk Report for February–March 2026
items.why_it_matters: Regular frontier-risk reporting improves external visibility into model capability, safeguards, and unresolved risk areas.

items.title: Study tests whether LLM deployments can worsen armed-conflict dynamics
items.why_it_matters: It targets a neglected safety domain: whether model outputs can amplify harm in fragile conflict contexts.

items.title: Sparse-feature audit links GPT-2 IOI failures to specific SAE activations
items.why_it_matters: Mechanistic audits could make model failures more diagnosable and support more targeted evaluations or interventions.

items.title: Commercial AI chatbots evaluated as news intermediaries across emerging events
items.why_it_matters: News-facing chatbot errors directly affect information reliability, especially across languages and fast-moving events.

items.title: Lumberjack proposes stronger differentially private random forests
items.why_it_matters: Better privacy-utility tradeoffs can make privacy-preserving ML more practical for sensitive tabular data.

items.title: LCGuard introduces a safeguard for latent KV-cache sharing in multi-agent systems
items.why_it_matters: Guarding hidden-agent communication addresses a growing attack and leakage surface in multi-agent architectures.

items.title: Distillation Game models adaptive model-stealing attacks and utility-constrained defenses
items.why_it_matters: It frames model extraction as a concrete security tradeoff between helpful outputs and imitation risk.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
